---
title: "DataCan"
subtitle: "Interactive metrics about Canada — population, economics, science & innovation"
---

DataCan provides interactive, regularly updated charts and downloadable data about Canada's key metrics. The goal is to make official statistics accessible, comparable, and useful for informed civic conversation.

All data comes from official sources — primarily [Statistics Canada](https://www.statcan.gc.ca/) and the [OECD](https://data.oecd.org/) — and is updated automatically. Where possible, Canada is shown alongside a broad set of OECD peer countries (not just the G7) to provide meaningful international context.

## Topics

### [Population](population/)
How many people live in Canada? How has the population been changing, and why? National and provincial estimates from Statistics Canada.

### [Science & Innovation](science/)
How much does Canada invest in research and development? How does this compare to peer countries? GERD as % of GDP from OECD MSTI data.

---

*This is an open, non-partisan project. It presents data without policy advocacy. Data files are freely downloadable for anyone to use.*
